_meta.track: A — past similar Kaggle simulation/agent comps
_meta.compiled_for: orbit-wars (kaggle_environments, 2D continuous-space conquest RTS, +1/-1 binary reward)
_meta.ordering: most-recent first; Halite II flagged as structurally most similar to orbit-wars

lux_ai_s3.structural_relevance: high — partial observability + best-of-5 meta-learning + 24x24 grid + sap actions
lux_ai_s3.architecture: 8-block 3x3 SE-ResNet, 256 hidden, ~10M params, dual actor heads (10 movement + spatial sap target), 10-frame history
lux_ai_s3.compute: 1x RTX 3090 + 1x RTX 2070S, 64GB RAM, AMD 9950X 16-core; ~110k env steps/s; ~430 train steps/s; ~300M frames over ~8 days (in-house Rust simulator)
key_tricks: Custom Rust simulator (TDD against official Python engine)
Hidden-info inference: deduce point-tile locations, asteroid/nebula movement, energy field params from observation deltas
Symmetry exploitation: reflect features across diagonal; test-time aug with diagonal+180 rotation
Selective action masking
Predicted asteroid/nebula motion as input feature

lux_ai_s2.structural_relevance: medium — discrete grid, but multi-unit factory placement + lichen growth (orbit-wars has resource generation similar to lichen)
lux_ai_s2.compute: modest — Python heuristic, no GPU training
lux_ai_s2.notes: Despite NeurIPS providing >1B frames of S1 imitation-learning data, the Kaggle leaderboard was won by a pure-Python rule-based bot. RL track was a separate scored evaluation. This is the canonical lesson: heuristic still beats RL on Kaggle leaderboards when env is complex and compute-budgeted.

lux_ai_s2_il_track.structural_relevance: the IL/RL stage; >1B frames of S1 play data provided by organizers; demonstrates IL + PPO finetune is viable but did not win the Kaggle bot LB

kore_2022.structural_relevance: high — fleet movement, resource collection, attack/defense, kaggle_environments backbone
kore_2022.architecture: Pixel-BERT-style multi-modal Transformer; 12-layer ResNet w/ GroupNorm encoder for 18-channel spatial; MLP for scalars; bag-of-words char embedding (256-d) for ship plans; autoregressive char-level decoder (commands like 'N 10 W 5')
kore_2022.training: 200M (obs, plan) tuples scraped from top-5 leaderboard submissions
kore_2022.compute: 2x A100 80GB, batch 64, 20 epochs, AdamW lr=4e-3 cosine, 5% warmup, gradclip 0.5, 60% spatial pixel dropout aug
key_tricks: Tokenize ship plan as character sequence (translation-style seq2seq)
[CLS] token -> action-type classifier head
Bag-of-words ship-plan embedding to avoid second Transformer cost
Aggressive 60% pixel dropout regularization on spatial input

halite_2.structural_relevance: MAXIMUM — 2D continuous-space, planet docking, fleet vs fleet melee, exact analog of orbit-wars
key_tricks: Fight-or-flight: sum nearby_enemies − nearby_allies in radius; if outnumbered, run vector = -mean(enemy_positions) (Coulomb-style 180° flee)
Iterative simulation refinement: test 19 enemy response patterns, choose conservative move
Multi-pass: strategic role assignment (colonize/defend/attack) then tactical hill-climbing on positions
Planet priority: 3 pts/dock-spot scaled by inverse distance; cutoff dist<75 in 2p, prefer outer planets in 4p
Anti-rush via simple thresholds (don't dock if enemy within X)
Ship pruning: only consider ships within (2*MAX_SPEED + WEAPON_RADIUS + 2*SHIP_RADIUS) of you
Desertion meta (4p): hide one ship in corner to outlive opponents and steal 2nd place
Non-aggression pact (shummie): hidden coordination signals between bots (controversial)
Distractor role: harass docked ships to split enemy defenses

halite_4.structural_relevance: medium — 4-player grid, but ship economy + combat nearly identical signature to orbit-wars
halite_4.top1_notes: Repo has separate 'Rule agents' and 'Deep Learning Agents' folders — final winner combined heuristic strategy with NN for opponent move prediction
halite_4.top4_notes: Triangle-shipyard 'plantation' farming, 100+ tunable params (later abandoned EA due to overfit), linear-sum-assignment for simultaneous ship moves, 10 ship roles (mining/returning/hunting/guarding/etc.)
halite_4.convex_team_notes: Centralized per-ship action prediction via semantic segmentation; heuristic overrides for spawn/convert/base protect

halite_3.structural_relevance: low — 2D grid, halite collection + dropoffs, less melee than orbit-wars
halite_3.top1_notes: Each ship runs Dijkstra to compute halite/turn for every square; chooses target maximizing gain rate. Collision threshold uses ownership × halite × ship-value. Dropoff scoring = halite_density / (cost − square_halite)
halite_3.place6_notes: Rewrote Python→C++ for speed; NN predicts opponent move probabilities; only commits move if ≥98% safe. Mining score = (dist_to + mining_time + return_dist) with 1.75x distance penalty

lux_ai_s1.structural_relevance: medium — discrete grid, day/night cycle adds temporal complexity
lux_ai_s1.architecture: fully convolutional ResNet w/ SE; 24 residual blocks, 128-channel 5x5; ~20M params; no batch-norm in residual blocks; 3 actor heads (workers/carts/city-tiles) + 1 critic
lux_ai_s1.compute: single 8c/16t personal PC w/ dual GPU; overnight training
key_tricks: GridNet action space (one network per grid cell)
Single network controls ALL units → emergent cooperation (vs. per-unit nets)
Reward shaping for first 20M steps then sparse +1/-1
Progressive net size 8→16→24 blocks with teacher distillation (KL)
Day/night and game-phase explicit input dims
Test-time augmentation: 180° rotation averaging
IMPALA + UPGO + TD(λ) loss, NOT plain PPO

hungry_geese.structural_relevance: low — 7x11 toroidal snake game, but illustrates AlphaZero/MCTS dominance
hungry_geese.top1_notes: CNN treats grid as torus; step-count channel for endgame strategy; mixed past versions during training (not pure self-play)
key_tricks: Continuous body gradient (1.0 head → 0.0 tail) instead of binary mask
Step-count channel as model input (essential late-game)
Mixed-version opponent pool (not vanilla self-play)
AlphaZero-style MCTS bolt-on at end with simultaneous-action + stochastic-food modifications

microrts_2023.compute: RAISocketAI 70 GPU-days (A10/A6000/A100); BC+PPO follow-up 142 GPU-days total (A10)
microrts_2023.notes: First DRL agent to win IEEE microRTS. BC+PPO matched 88% win rate without per-map specialization. PPO struggled on largest 64x64 map; authors suggest A2C as smoother BC→DRL transition.
key_tricks: DoubleCone(4,6,4) architecture w/ SE blocks + adaptive pooling for variable map sizes
Curriculum: 16x16 → 32x32 transfer-finetune → 64x64 (94% of win came from 16x16, 64x64 marginal)
Cross-entropy BC loss SCALED BY UNIT COUNT (allowed much higher LR)
Mixed reward schedule: dense early → sparse +1/-1 late
Invalid action masking 'essential to compete at basic level'

when_heuristic_won: Halite IV 1st (ttvand, 2020) — heuristic core w/ NN collision aid
Lux AI S2 1st (ry-andy, 2023) — pure Python heuristic beat IL/RL competitors
Kore 2022 leaderboard prior to IL submission
when_imitation_learning_won: Kore 2022 1st (khanhvu207) — autoregressive Transformer on 200M tuples from top-5 IL targets
_cross_comp_synthesis.trend: Until 2023 the Kaggle simulation LB was dominated by heuristic. With Lux S3 (2024) DRL won on a partial-observability + meta-learning game where heuristic could not encode all hidden dynamics. orbit-wars is fully observable + symmetric, which favors heuristic baselines, but the 2-month timeline + 2370 teams favors hybrid (heuristic core + ML for opponent prediction or policy distillation).
transferable_recipe_for_orbit_wars: Phase 1 (week 1-2): pure heuristic w/ fight-or-flight + planet priority queue (Halite II reCurs3 pattern). Cite: recursive.cc.
Phase 2 (week 3-4): add NN opponent move predictor for combat decisions (Halite III TheDuck314 pattern + 98% safety threshold).
Phase 3 (week 5-6): scrape top-N leaderboard replays, BC autoregressive policy on (obs, action) tuples (Kore 2022 pattern, 60% spatial dropout aug).
Phase 4 (week 7-8): PPO finetune w/ per-unit cross-entropy loss scaled by unit count (microRTS 2023 trick).
Mandatory tricks: action masking, symmetry test-time aug (4-fold around (50,50)), ship pruning by interaction radius.
